what: Linked Plot Maps for Multivariate High-Resolution Spatiotemporal Data
title: Linked Plot Maps for Multivariate High-Resolution Spatiotemporal Data
text: goal DrivePlotR produce linked plot maps multivariate high-resolution spatiotemporal data vehicle trajectories naturalistic driving studies connected vehicles.

title: Linked Plot Maps for Multivariate High-Resolution Spatiotemporal Data
text: can install DrivePlotR CRAN : can install development version DrivePlotR GitHub :

title: Linked Plot Maps for Multivariate High-Resolution Spatiotemporal Data
text: example shows basic workflow visualizing vehicle trajectories DrivePlotR. Load DrivePlotR make drive data available R session. made non-sensitive drives naturalistic driving study available part DrivePlotR package, data geographic temporal components also suitable. row data must represent one observation vehicle trajectory. Establish geographic variables projection. Setting geographic projection WGS84 ensures data points compatible Leaflet maps. already done example dataset drive7 included package. Convert drive data shared data frame. Create visualizations. Although main purpose DrivePlotR create linked visualizations map one four companion graphs, users can also create maps companion graphs separately. can create basic standalone map following code: can also create standalone companion graph follows: DrivePlotR offers many options customization. example, following code creates complex linked plot map. companion graphs include speed, gyroscopic heading (direction vehicle moving), GPS position dilution precision (PDOP,measure quality GPS location data). points map companion graphs colored minute drive occurred. DrivePlotR provides users straightforward way transform data columns right specified. example , speed miles per hour (MPH) converted kilometers per hour (KPH), gyro heading numbers translated general directions (N, S, E, W). activate selection box map, click selection button +/- zoom buttons upper left corner map. selection box appear map. can move selection box clicking 9 dots upper left corner selection box dragging selection box desired location. Use white squares corners selection box resize box. points fall within selection box map also selected companion graphs. remove selection box map, click X button +/- zoom buttons upper left corner map. clear corresponding selection companion graphs, double-click anywhere within plotting area one companion graphs. see interaction tools companion graphs, hover top first companion graph stack. modebar selection buttons appear. left right, buttons : Zoom, Pan, Box Select, Lasso Select, Zoom , Zoom , Autoscale, Reset axes. Click button interact plot map. Points select companion graphs also selected map. Double-click anywhere within plotting area one companion graphs reset companion graphs.
code: library(DrivePlotR) data("drive7") shared_drive <- crosstalk::SharedData$new(drive7) driveplot_map(   shareddata = shared_drive ) driveplot_companion(   shareddata = shared_drive,   x = time_cst,   y = speed_mph,   xlabel = "Time",   ylabel = "Speed (MPH)" ) driveplot(   shareddata = shared_drive,   x = time_cst,   ys = c(speed_mph, gyro_heading, gps_pdop),   colorvar = gps_minute,   maplabel = time_cst,   colorpalette = "viridis",   xlabel = "Time",   ylabels = c("Speed (MPH)", "Gyro Heading", "GPS PDOP"),   showlegend = TRUE,   legendtitle = "Minute",   plottitle = "A Drive in Omaha, NE" ) driveplot(   shareddata = shared_drive,   x = time_cst,   ys = c(     speed_mph * 1.60934,     c("N", "E", "S", "W")[findInterval(       (gyro_heading + 45) %% 360,       c(90, 180, 270)     ) + 1],     gps_pdop   ),   colorvar = gps_minute,   maplabel = time_cst,   colorpalette = "viridis",   xlabel = "Time",   ylabels = c("Speed (KPH)", "General Gyro Heading", "GPS PDOP"),   showlegend = TRUE,   legendtitle = "Minute",   plottitle = "A Drive in Omaha, NE" )

what: Blackbox-Driving Data for one drive — drive7
title: Blackbox-Driving Data for one drive — drive7
text: dataset contains drive 7 dataset nds_data.

title: Blackbox-Driving Data for one drive — drive7
Blackbox-Driving Data for one drive — drive7
text: tibble 321 rows 34 columns drive integer, identifier drive; drive defined time starting car turning . time_utc POSIXct time stamp observation UTC time_cst POSIXct time stamp observation Central time GPS-based measurements observation: gps_elevation numeric, elevation sea-level (m) gps_heading numeric, continuity-corrected direction car (degrees north 0 degrees increasing clockwise) measured GPS. continuity correction takes initial GPS heading adds cumulative sum changes GPS heading subsequent observations. gps_fix numeric, value specifying adequacy GPS fix, 0 = fix, 1 = time , 2 = 2D fix, 3 = 3D fix. gps_fix 3 needed good GPS data. gps_sats integer, number satellites covering position. gps_pdop numeric, position dilution precision, overall GPS data quality measure (lower better) gps_hdop numeric, horizontal dilution precision, GPS quality measure latitude/longitude data (lower better) gps_vdop numeric, vertical dilution precision, GPS quality measure elevation data (lower better) speed_mph numeric, speed vehicle (miles per hour) speed_source character, source reported speed, either "gps" "obd" distance_miles numeric, distance traveled drive (miles) Measurements -board gyroscope: accel_x numeric, measure vehicle acceleration along front-back (longitudinal) axis (g) accel_y numeric, measure vehicle acceleration along right-left (lateral) axis (g) accel_z numeric, measure acceleration vehicle -plane (normal/yaw axis) (g) accel_event numeric, sequential (time) variable marking consecutive acceleration values (absolute value accel_x/y) exceed threshold 0.35g minimum 1 second accel_event_cat character, severity categorization acceleration event low (0.35-0.44g), medium (0.45-0.59g), high (>0.6g) gyro_x numeric, angular rotation rate right--left axis (degrees/sec) gyro_y numeric, angular rotation rate forward--backward axis (degrees/sec) gyro_z numeric, angular rotation rate --axis (degrees/sec) gyro_heading numeric, calculated heading utilizing compass data gyro data (degrees north 0 degrees increasing clockwise). Note use non-standard congruence classes continuity consecutive values. Due errors raw gyro heading, use initial GPS heading value initial heading value subtract cumulative sum gyro_z subsequent observations calculate gyro_heading. grav_x numeric, lateral gravity (g) grav_y numeric, longitudinal gravity (g) grav_z numeric, gravity (-) (g) roll numeric, angle forward--backward axis. 0 = level, positive = tilted left (degrees) pitch numeric, angle left--right axis 0 = level, positive = tilted downward (.e., vehicle going downhill) (degrees) engine_rpm numeric, current engine revolutions per minute value engine_throttle numeric, engine throttle percentage (0-100%) cumulative_drive_dist_mi numeric, cumulative distance driven miles observation gps_minute factor, minute component timestamp observation (00 59) Raw heading measurements: gps_heading_raw numeric, raw GPS heading without continuity correction (degrees) gyro_heading_raw numeric, raw gyro heading without continuity correction (degrees) GPS coordinates simple features column: geometry list-column, simple features geometry column geometry type POINT CRS WGS84

title: Create a vehicle trajectory plot map — driveplot
code: driveplot(   shareddata,   lng = NULL,   lat = NULL,   x,   ys,   colorvar = NULL,   maplabel = NA,   colorpalette = NULL,   fillopacity = 1,   xlabel = NULL,   ylabels = NULL,   showlegend = TRUE,   legendtitle = NULL,   plottitle = NULL,   spacing = 0.05,   width = "100%",   height = "100vh" )

title: Create a vehicle trajectory plot map — driveplot
text: shareddata SharedData object containing observations plotted. lng bare (unquoted) column shareddata containing longitude (required shareddata geometry column). lat bare (unquoted) column shareddata containing latitude (required shareddata geometry column) x bare (unquoted) column shareddata plotted horizontal axis. ys vector list bare (unquoted) columns shareddata plotted vertical axes companion graphs. colorvar bare (unquoted) column shareddata color mapped. maplabel optional label map points. colorpalette color palette plot; either single color (e.g., "red") one viridis color palettes compatible leaflet. Run leaflet_color_palettes() see available options viridis color palettes. colorvar colorpalette provided, colorpalette defaults blue. colorvar provided colorpalette , colorpalette defaults color palette "viridis". fillopacity opacity fill map points (0 1). xlabel label variable horizontal axis. ylabels vector list labels vertical axes companion graphs. provided, length ys. showlegend Show plot legend (TRUE) (FALSE). legendtitle title plot legend. plottitle title plot map. spacing value 0 1 space companion graphs. width width plot map, provided string one following units: %, vh, vw, px (e.g., "100%" "400px") height height plot map, provided string one following units: %, vh, vw, px (e.g., "100%" "400px")

what: Guess latitude and longitude column names Helper function for internal use only. Adapted from the leaflet function guessLatLongCols. Rather than looking for the exact variable names lat or latitude, the regular expression allows for variables that start or end with lat/latitude for latitude; similarly for longitude. — guess_lat_long_cols

Central time GPS-based measurements observation: gps_long numeric, geographic longitude degrees CRS WGS84 gps_lat numeric, geographic latitude degrees CRS WGS84 gps_elevation numeric, elevation sea-level (m) gps_heading numeric, continuity-corrected direction car (degrees north 0 degrees increasing clockwise) measured GPS. continuity correction takes initial GPS heading adds cumulative sum changes GPS heading subsequent observations. gps_fix numeric, value specifying adequacy GPS fix, 0 = fix, 1 = time , 2 = 2D fix, 3 = 3D fix. gps_fix 3 needed good GPS data. gps_sats integer, number latitude/longitude data (lower better) gps_vdop numeric, vertical dilution precision, GPS quality measure elevation data (lower better) speed_mph numeric, speed vehicle (miles per hour) speed_source front-back (longitudinal) axis (g) accel_y numeric, measure vehicle acceleration along right-left (lateral) axis (g) accel_z numeric, measure acceleration vehicle -plane (normal/yaw axis) (g) accel_event numeric, sequential (time) variable marking consecutive acceleration values (absolute value accel_x/y) exceed threshold 0.35g minimum 1 second accel_event_cat character, severity categorization acceleration event low (0.35-0.44g), medium (0.45-0.59g), high (>0.6g) gyro_x numeric, angular rotation rate right--left axis (degrees/sec) gyro_y numeric, angular rotation rate forward--backward axis (degrees/sec) gyro_z numeric, angular rotation rate --axis (degrees/sec) gyro_heading numeric, calculated heading utilizing compass data gyro data (degrees north 0 degrees increasing clockwise). Note use non-standard congruence classes continuity consecutive values. Due errors raw gyro heading, use initial GPS heading value initial heading value subtract cumulative sum gyro_z subsequent observations calculate gyro_heading. grav_x numeric, lateral gravity (g) grav_y numeric, longitudinal gravity (g) grav_z numeric, gravity (-) (g) roll numeric, angle forward--backward axis. 0 = level, positive = tilted left (degrees) pitch numeric, angle left--right axis 0 = level, positive = tilted downward (.e., vehicle going downhill) (degrees) engine_rpm numeric, current engine revolutions per minute value engine_throttle numeric, engine throttle percentage (0-100%) cumulative_drive_dist_mi numeric, cumulative distance driven miles observation gps_minute factor, minute component timestamp observation (00 59) Raw heading measurements: gps_heading_raw numeric, raw GPS heading without continuity correction (degrees) gyro_heading_raw numeric, raw gyro heading without continuity correction (degrees)
